The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS DORAN MCGEE AND MARINA GABRIEL'S Motion To Enforce This Courts March 16, 2022 Order; Request For Issue And Evidence Sanctions Against Defendant Sandra McGee And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Sandra McGee And Her Attorney Of Record, Cathy Elliott Jones, In The Amount Of $3,972.

Motion GRANTED in part, as follows. Verified responses to discovery to be served no later than August 10, 2022. Evidence or issue sanctions are not warranted because Defendants, through their new counsel, belatedly agreed to provide responses, and granting such sanctions would put Plaintiffs in a better position than if they received the discovery. The prior order by JPT Cohen did not require the appointment of a guardian ad litem, and in any event, Defendants' new counsel indicates that there is a substitute trustee. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in part. Defendants' new counsel substituted in after the motion was filed so the motion appears reasonably necessary. Sanctions granted in the amount of $4,581 (original sanctions of $1,259 plus $3,250 for 5 hours at $650/hour, plus filing costs of $72) payable by August 10, 2022. CRC 3.1348 (permitting sanctions even after discovery provided).

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sborrowman@gmail.com.
